headlines.title: BRIDGE APPROACH NEARS COMPLETION
headlines.subtitle: Twelfth Street Structure May Be Opened to Traf- fic Oct. 14.

Though this has not been decided. It is in such condition now that an of- fleer is kept there to prevent its over- bonding. "Drive Blow" signs are on the Samuels avenue bridge.

headlines.title: PLAN BIG SUM FOR GEORGETOWN COLLEGE
headlines.subtitle: Methodists Want to Raise $300,000 fer Southwestern University.
headlines.fullText: GEORGETOWN, Texas, The Texas Methodist educational com- mission has decided to start a cam- Bauen to raise $300. 000 for the South- western University at Georgetown. Of this amount $200,000 will be for en- dowment and $100.000 for two new buildings. The work will start Dec. 1.

headlines.title: Artist of 'Smiling Lincoln,' 93, Still Busy in Studio
headlines.fullText: By Associated Press, NEW YORK, Sept. 25 .- Alhan Jas par Conant, the painter, for whom 1.th. coln sat for.a portrait before fit; be came President, celebrated his ninety third birthday here yesterday in the Tenth street studio Wien ner cupled more man trenerde In And every day finds him but with brush and palette. Mr. Conant knew Lincoln personally both at Springfield, IIL, where, in 110 Point and at Washington, where dur ing the Civil war he had commission to paint many cabinet officers ata notables of the day. Mr. Conant was born in Chelsea, VE Sept. 24, 1821. His father was a houm and sign painter.

headlines.title: DEFLECTED BULLET KILLS BYSTANDER
headlines.subtitle: Chicago Policeman Fires at Robber, Hits Trolley Pole and Man
headlines.fullText: william Allen at three highwaymes early today, atruck an manwapen deflected and killed William Lorimer, a tobacco agent. Three friends, talking with Larimer when he was shot, setted the police- man ifeil took him to the station, where he was placed under arrest. Another policeman heard the shots and captured one of the robbers.
